subject: Accounting
title: External vs. Internal Auditors
description: A quick win: understand External vs. Internal Auditors.

hook.text: External auditors report to shareholders. Internal auditors report to the company itself.

buildup.text: External auditors are independent outsiders hired to provide an objective opinion on the books.

discovery.text: Internal auditors work inside the company, testing controls and looking for risks year-round.

twist.text: Cynthia Cooper was an internal auditor. She found WorldCom's $3.8 billion fraud from the inside.

climax.text: Both roles are essential. Externals provide credibility. Internals catch problems early.

punchline.text: Outside eyes give trust. Inside eyes give warning.

quiz.question: Who do external auditors report to?
